skill.name: agent-manager-skill
skill.description: Manage multiple local CLI agents through tmux sessions, task assignment, monitoring, and cron-friendly recurring work.
skill.summary: Coordinate local CLI agents with tmux sessions, monitoring, task assignment, and scheduled runs.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The Ruby shell backtick and weak cryptography alerts are false positives caused by Markdown backticks and the word cron in SKILL.md. The meaningful risk is that the skill instructs users to clone an external GitHub repository and run Python scripts from it, which is legitimate for an agent manager but requires trust in unaudited external code.

medium_findings.title: External Repository Execution Workflow
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs users to clone https://github.com/fractalmind-ai/agent-manager-skill.git and then run python3 agent-manager scripts. This is useful for the stated purpose, but the executable Python code is not included in the audited skill package, so users must trust external code before running agent management commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly shown in SKILL.md and the workflow clearly depends on running external Python scripts. There is no evidence of malicious intent, but the unaudited execution path is a real supply chain risk.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby shell backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code and fenced code blocks, not executable Ruby code. The code block contains shell commands, but the backticks themselves do not execute anything in this skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file content shows Markdown formatting around installation and command examples. No Ruby file or Ruby execution context is present.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm alert points to the description line, which only says the skill is cron-friendly. No cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic API use appears at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains descriptive metadata and no cryptographic operation. The finding appears to be a keyword collision rather than a security issue.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub URL Requires Trust Review
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is a public GitHub clone command, not evidence of data exfiltration. It still creates a dependency on an external repository that can change outside this audited package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network reference is visible and limited to a GitHub clone URL. The risk is supply chain exposure, not confirmed malicious network behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Clone And Execute External Tooling
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs users to clone an external repository and run Python commands from the cloned agent-manager tooling. This pattern can be safe when the repository is trusted, but it should be disclosed because execution happens outside the audited SKILL.md file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The install and run commands are explicit. The exact behavior of the external Python scripts cannot be confirmed from this skill package.

content.value_statement: Teams that run several CLI agents need a repeatable way to start, assign, and monitor local work. This skill provides command guidance for tmux-based agent sessions and scheduled recurring tasks.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install the agent manager repository with git clone.
Documents a doctor command for checking the local agent manager setup.
Provides commands to list agents and start a named agent session.
Shows how to monitor a named agent with follow mode enabled.
Provides an example for assigning a multi-line task to an agent.
States prerequisites for tmux, python3, and an agents directory.
limitations: The executable agent manager scripts are not included in the audited skill file.
Users must review and trust the external GitHub repository before running commands.
The skill does not document authentication, sandboxing, or permission controls.
Cron scheduling is mentioned, but no full cron configuration example is included.

use_cases.title: Run parallel coding agents
use_cases.description: Start separate tmux sessions for local CLI agents and monitor their progress from the terminal.

use_cases.title: Assign maintenance tasks
use_cases.description: Send structured task instructions to a named agent and follow its output while it works.

use_cases.title: Schedule recurring agent work
use_cases.description: Use the documented cron-friendly workflow as a starting point for repeated local agent operations.

prompt_templates.title: Check setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agent-manager-skill guidance to help me check whether my local agent manager setup is ready.

prompt_templates.title: Start one agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agent-manager-skill workflow to start a local CLI agent named EMP_0001 and explain what each command does.

prompt_templates.title: Assign and monitor work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agent-manager-skill workflow to assign a maintenance task to EMP_0002 and monitor the agent output.

prompt_templates.title: Plan scheduled operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agent-manager-skill guidance to design a cron-friendly plan for recurring local agent maintenance work.

output_examples.input: Check whether my agent manager setup is ready.
output_examples.output: The response lists the setup check command, explains the tmux and python3 prerequisites, and notes that agents are configured under an agents directory.

output_examples.input: Assign a task to EMP_0002 and watch progress.
output_examples.output: The response gives a concise terminal workflow for sending the task text to the named agent and following its output.

output_examples.input: Plan recurring maintenance with local agents.
output_examples.output: The response outlines a cron-friendly workflow and highlights the need to review the external agent manager repository first.

best_practices: Review the external repository before running its Python scripts.
Use clear agent names and keep task instructions specific.
Run setup checks before starting or scheduling agent sessions.
anti_patterns: Do not run cloned scripts from an unreviewed repository.
Do not assign broad tasks without defining expected outputs.
Do not schedule recurring commands before confirming local prerequisites.

faq.question: What does this skill help manage?
faq.answer: It helps manage local CLI agents through tmux sessions, task assignment, monitoring, and scheduled workflows.

faq.question: Does the skill include the agent manager code?
faq.answer: No. It instructs users to clone an external repository that contains the agent manager scripts.

faq.question: What tools are required?
faq.answer: The skill states that tmux and python3 are required.

faq.question: Can it monitor agent output?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents a monitor command with follow mode for a named agent.

faq.question: Can it assign tasks to agents?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows a multi-line task assignment pattern for a named agent.

faq.question: Is it safe to run immediately?
faq.answer: Review the external repository first because the executable scripts are outside the audited skill package.
